# UC-22.55.7 · CLC/TS 50701 Target Security Levels (SL-T) Coverage — every zone has an SL-T justified against threat scenarios

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*For every zone of our railway, we set how strong its cyber-protection should be — like setting how many locks each door needs. There are 7 categories (passwords, what users can do, data integrity, data secrecy, network restrictions, response time, system availability) and we set each one 1–4.*

---

## Description

Verifies every zone has all 7 IEC 62443-3-3 FR SL-T values set with current justification.

## Value

SL-T is the contract between risk assessment and downstream SR/CR work. An incomplete SL-T register cascades into mis-aligned controls. This UC ensures completeness.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `rail_sl_register_lookup`. (2) Risk Manager + Signal Engineering Lead jointly set SL-T per zone with documented justification. (3) Schedule UC-22.55.7 weekly Monday 04:30.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.55.7` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `clc_clause="CLC-TS-50701-c7-1"`. Every row pins (zone_id, sl_target_overall, sl_t_AC, sl_t_UC, sl_t_DI, sl_t_TRE, sl_t_RDF, sl_t_RA, sl_t_RA, sl_t_set_at, sl_t_justification, sl_t_status). Dashboard `CLC/TS 50701 — SL-T Coverage` shows SL-T currency.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Set `sl_t_AC` to null for a high-risk zone (e.g., RBC zone). UC-22.55.7 MUST flag `sl_t_status="slt_incomplete"`.

### Negative scenario

Set all 7 SL-T fields (AC, UC, DI, TRE, RDF, RA, TRE) to defined values 1-4 with justification documents. UC-22.55.7 MUST record `sl_t_status="current"`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- KV Store `rail_sl_register_lookup`.

## Step 1 — SL register collection

```ini
[rail_sl_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.zone_id = string
field.zone_status = string
field.sl_t_AC = number
field.sl_t_UC = number
field.sl_t_DI = number
field.sl_t_TRE = number
field.sl_t_RDF = number
field.sl_t_RA = number
field.sl_t_TRC = number
field.sl_t_set_at = number
field.sl_t_justification = string
```

IEC 62443-3-3 foundational requirements (FRs):
- AC = Identification and authentication control
- UC = Use control
- DI = System integrity
- TRE = Data confidentiality
- RDF = Restricted data flow
- RA = Timely response to events
- TRC = Resource availability

## Step 2 — Search and alert

```ini
[UC-22.55.7: CLC/TS 50701 SL-T Coverage]
cron_schedule = 30 4 * * 1
dispatch.earliest_time = -3y@y
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.55.7,reg=CLC-TS-50701,clause=CLC-TS-50701-c7-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = Rail SL-T incomplete or missing
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Operationalize

**Runbook for `slt_incomplete` or `slt_missing`** (owner: Risk Manager, severity P2):

1. Within 60 days: complete SL-T determination with Engineering and document.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

**CLC/TS 50701-specific failure modes**:

- **High SL-T zones often have high CAPEX implications**: documenting SL-T high creates implementation pressure; this is by design.
- **IEC 62443-3-3 vs IEC 62443-4-2 mapping**: SL-T drives SR (zone-level, UC-22.55.8) and CR (component-level, UC-22.55.9).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup rail_sl_register_lookup
| where zone_status="active"
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval slt_age_months = if(isnotnull(sl_t_set_at), round((now_ts - sl_t_set_at) / 86400 / 30, 1), null())
| eval slt_completeness = if(isnotnull(sl_t_AC) AND isnotnull(sl_t_UC) AND isnotnull(sl_t_DI) AND isnotnull(sl_t_TRE) AND isnotnull(sl_t_RDF) AND isnotnull(sl_t_RA) AND isnotnull(sl_t_TRC), 1, 0)
| eval sl_t_status = case(
    isnull(sl_t_set_at), "slt_missing",
    slt_completeness=0, "slt_incomplete",
    slt_age_months > 24, "slt_stale",
    isnull(sl_t_justification) OR sl_t_justification="", "justification_missing",
    1=1, "current")
| where sl_t_status != "current"
| table zone_id sl_t_AC sl_t_UC sl_t_DI sl_t_TRE sl_t_RDF sl_t_RA sl_t_TRC sl_t_set_at slt_age_months sl_t_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) SL-T heatmap (zone vs FR). (Panel 2) Justification-coverage. (Panel 3) Stale-zone queue.

## Known False Positives

1. **Newly-defined zone** — 90-day commissioning grace.

2. **Single-FR omission accepted** — in rare cases a specific FR may be NA for a zone; documented as `sl_t_FR=NA` with justification.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `clc_22_55_7_exceptions` with documented Risk Manager approval and 60-day maximum grant.

## References

- [IEC 62443-3-3 — System security requirements and security levels](https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/7033)
- [CLC/TS 50701:2021 clause 7.1](https://standards.cencenelec.eu/dyn/www/f?p=205:110:0::::FSP_ORG_ID,FSP_PROJECT:1258376,73987)
